name: J. David Tax Law
description_short: Tax attorney firm specializing in IRS debt resolution, tax relief programs, and enforcement action defense across multiple states with offices in Philadelphia, PA.
description_long:
J. David Tax Law is a multi-state tax law firm headquartered in Philadelphia, PA, with offices across Florida, California, Texas, North Carolina, Arizona, Georgia, Maryland, New York, and Washington, DC. The firm has over four decades of collective expertise in federal and state tax resolution. The company markets itself as having saved taxpayers over $800 million through successful resolution of audits, back taxes, liens, levies, and tax disputes. They position themselves as specialists in resolving complex tax issues for residents struggling with IRS compliance and state tax authority problems.

The firm offers a comprehensive suite of tax relief services including Offer in Compromise (settling tax debt for less owed), IRS Fresh Start Program qualification, Currently Not Collectible status, penalty and interest relief, First Time Penalty Abatement, and IRS Payment Plans. They also handle enforcement actions including wage garnishment release, tax levy removal, tax lien resolution, asset seizure defense, and IRS appeals. Additional services cover tax audit representation, IRS criminal investigation defense, innocent spouse relief, payroll tax issues (940/941 forms), and unfiled tax return resolution.

The firm distinguishes itself through its stated $800 million in taxpayer savings, multi-state presence with local offices, and claimed expertise in both federal IRS and state tax authority matters. They emphasize understanding of Philadelphia's high local tax burden and provide free consultations with a quoted phone number for immediate contact. Their website highlights awards, recognition, case studies, and testimonials as social proof of their track record.

However, the website makes broad claims without specific verification, such as the $800 million savings figure and thousands of successful resolutions. No independent licensing verification, client reviews, or detailed case outcomes are provided on the site itself. The firm's categorization as "bankruptcy" appears incorrect—this is primarily a tax law firm, though tax debt can be addressed through bankruptcy. Consumers should verify attorney credentials independently and understand that tax resolution outcomes vary significantly based on individual circumstances.
pros: Multi-state presence with 15+ office locations including major cities (NYC, LA, San Francisco, Houston, Miami, etc.)
Claimed $800 million in aggregate taxpayer savings through resolved audits, liens, levies, and disputes
Offers free consultation and free 4-step qualification assessment with no apparent upfront cost
Specializes in both federal IRS enforcement actions (wage garnishments, asset seizure, levies) and state tax authority issues
Comprehensive service menu covering 20+ specific tax relief programs and strategies (OIC, Fresh Start, Currently Not Collectible, etc.)
Advertises 40+ years collective team expertise in federal and state tax law
Serves all 50 states despite geographic office locations
cons: $800 million savings claim lacks independent verification or detailed case documentation on website
No transparent pricing model, fee structure, or cost estimates provided on website
Limited independent reviews or third-party validation visible on site; relies primarily on internal testimonials
Website does not specify attorney credentials, bar admissions, or individual qualifications beyond general "seasoned tax attorneys" language
Category miscoding as 'bankruptcy' when this is fundamentally a tax law firm (tax debt resolution differs from bankruptcy filing services)
best_for: Self-employed individuals and small business owners facing IRS wage garnishment, levy, or asset seizure actions
Philadelphia-area residents with Pennsylvania state tax debt or high local wage tax burdens
Taxpayers seeking Offer in Compromise or Fresh Start Program qualification with professional representation
High-income earners facing IRS audit, criminal investigation, or innocent spouse relief needs
diagnosis: J. David Tax Law is best for taxpayers facing aggressive IRS collection actions (garnishment, levy, asset seizure) or state tax debt who want professional representation to negotiate relief programs. The main caveat is that broad claims on the website lack independent verification, pricing is undisclosed, and outcomes depend heavily on individual financial circumstances—consumers should verify attorney credentials independently and understand that not all taxpayers qualify for programs like Offer in Compromise.
services: Offer in Compromise (settle tax debt for less than owed)
IRS Fresh Start Program qualification and relief
Currently Not Collectible status filing and management
IRS penalty and interest relief and elimination
IRS Payment Plan setup and negotiation
Tax audit representation and dispute resolution
